qa_pairs.question: How can you confirm if a Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injection file is working correctly in the Ansys Fluent graphical user interface?
qa_pairs.answer: After selecting an injection file and returning to the "Injections" dialog box in Ansys Fluent, you should see the specified injection name and be able to list its particles and properties in the console. No error or warning messages should appear in the console if the file is working properly.

qa_pairs.question: How do you enable updating Discrete Phase Model (DPM) source terms during a Fluent simulation?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable updating DPM source terms every flow iteration in Fluent, open the Discrete Phase Model Dialog Box and select "Interaction with Continuous Phase". This setting can be saved in your case file or added via the journal file.

qa_pairs.question: What are example journal file commands to automate the setup and interaction of Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injections in Ansys Fluent?
qa_pairs.answer: After solution initialization, you can use journal file commands such as `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes` to enable coupled calculations, and `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no` to create an injection from a file. Commands like `/define/models/dpm/injections/list-particles injection-0:1` and `/define/models/dpm/injections/list-injection-properties injection-0:1` can be used to list particle and injection properties, respectively.
